message:
fix(mysql): Handle TCP fragmentation in async packet I/O

Fixed several bugs in the MySQL async connection related to TCP stream
behavior:

1. Header reads now loop until all 4 bytes are received - TCP can return
   partial data on any read, so we can't assume a single read gets the
   full header.

2. Continuation packet header reads similarly loop for completeness in
   multi-packet handling (packets > 16MB).

3. Continuation payload reads now return an error on EOF instead of
   silently using partial data.

4. Write operations now loop to handle partial writes - poll_write() can
   return fewer bytes than requested, consistent with write_packet_raw_async().

5. Fixed field name: column_type instead of field_type on ColumnDef.

6. Added #[allow(dead_code)] for statement_id metadata field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
